fields.description:
About Us:

Here at Ambience, we never set out to be just another scribe. We’re building the AI intelligence platform that restores humanity to healthcare and drives meaningful ROI for health systems across the country.

Our technology helps providers focus on delivering great care by removing the administrative burden that pulls them away from patients and away from their most impactful work. Ambience delivers real-time coding-aware documentation and clinical workflow support across ambulatory, emergency and inpatient settings at the top health systems in North America.

Our teams operate relentlessly with extreme ownership to build the best solutions for our health system partners. We value candor, positivity and deep thought — and we expect a lot from each other because we know the problems we’re solving truly matter.

Ambience was ranked #1 for Improving the Clinician Experience in the KLAS Research Emerging Solutions Top 20 Report, recognized by Fast Company as one of the Next Big Things in Tech, named one of the best AI companies in healthcare by Inc., and selected as a LinkedIn Top Startup in 2024 and 2025. We’re backed by Oak HC/FT, Andreessen Horowitz (a16z), OpenAI Startup Fund, and Kleiner Perkins — and we’re just getting started.


THE ROLE

Reports to: Ambience President, Co-Founder, and Chairman - Mike Ng

Work Location: San Francisco HQ (Hybrid, 3+ days onsite)

The Office of the President exists to answer Ambience’s hardest questions: where the company is going, how it wins its category, and what must be true for Ambience to become the defining healthcare AI platform by 2030.

This office is not a functional team and not an additional management layer. It is a problem‑solving nucleus built to help the President see the future more clearly, architect the best possible version of Ambience, and move the company decisively toward it. The Chief of Staff is the core operating partner within that office.

In this role, you help drive trajectory‑changing decisions - the choices that materially shape Ambience’s long‑term direction, ecosystem position, and competitive advantage. These include decisions around platform evolution, strategic partnerships, market sequencing, external relationships, and moments where speed, judgment, and leverage matter more than consensus.

You do not own strategy in a vacuum, nor do you operate with predefined lanes. Instead, you take the beginnings of direction from the President – a working hypothesis, a voice memo, a conversation – and turn it into concrete progress: framing the problem, gathering signal, aligning the right people, and driving execution until a real decision or outcome is reached.

Some weeks you will quarterback multi‑threaded initiatives across senior leaders and external partners. Other weeks, you will personally own and execute critical work end‑to‑end. Priorities will shift. Timelines will compress. The work will often be confidential, urgent, and high‑stakes.

This role is for someone who genuinely wants to be at the tip of the spear – working on the most important things in the company, at the moment they matter most.


WHAT YOU’LL ACCOMPLISH

President Leverage & Decision Support

 - Act as the President’s strategic and operational right hand on the company’s most critical initiatives

 - Translate high-level direction (e.g., voice memos, working sessions, early hypotheses and rough ideas) into clear plans, decisions, and execution

 - Prepare pre-reads, frameworks, and analyses that directly inform executive decision-making

Trajectory-Changing Initiatives

 - Own or quarterback the most important projects in the company, often with little notice

 - Lead fast-moving, high-stakes workstreams involving senior internal leaders and external executives (e.g., hyperscalers, foundation model partners, health system CEOs)

 - Navigate confidential, time-sensitive initiatives including partnerships, negotiations, and strategic pivots

Cross-Functional Orchestration

 - Bring structure, momentum, and accountability to complex, multi-threaded efforts

 - Align stakeholders across Product, Engineering, GTM, Legal, Finance, and external partners

 - Identify bottlenecks early and creatively remove them

Problem Solving Under Ambiguity

 - Take loosely defined problems and impose clarity, sequencing, and decision logic

 - Rapidly gather signal, test hypotheses, and adapt direction as new information emerges

 - Operate comfortably across altitudes – from executive narrative to detailed execution


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Must Have

 - 5+ years of experience in consulting, strategy, BizOps, investing, or high-impact operating roles

 - Proven ability to solve ambiguous, high-stakes problems with limited direction

 - Exceptional clarity of thought and communication (especially written)

 - Experience working closely with senior executives or founders

 - High judgment, discretion, and comfort handling confidential information

Strong Signals

 - You are a fast learner who actively seeks out new information and updates your views as you learn – while also valuing feedback, debate, and mentorship from strong partners

 - You are motivated by getting to the truth, not by being right; you enjoy having your assumptions challenged, are comfortable saying “I don’t know,” and move quickly to find out

 - You use writing, analysis, and action as tools to create clarity and drive decisions

 - You follow problems through to real outcomes, even when the work is unglamorous or cross-functional

 - You operate with low ego and high integrity, prioritizing what’s best for the company over personal scope

 - You are highly AI-forward: you actively use AI systems to think, learn, analyze, and communicate, operating at roughly a Level 4 AI Fluency (AI as a thinking partner, not just a productivity hack)

Nice to Have

 - Healthcare, enterprise SaaS, AI, or startup experience

 - Experience in partnerships, corporate development, or complex negotiations

 - Experience operating in AI-native environments where decisions are informed by first-principles reasoning, rapid learning loops, and written synthesis (e.g., memos, briefs, or decision docs that materially shaped outcomes)


WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

This role sits at the center of Ambience’s most important work. You will have unmatched exposure to executive decision-making, external ecosystem dynamics, and the hardest problems the company faces.

It is also a demanding role – requiring flexibility, resilience, and a genuine desire to serve the mission above personal scope or autonomy. For the right person, it will be the fastest learning environment in the company and one of the most impactful roles of their career.


PAY TRANSPARENCY

The base compensation for this role is approximately $165,000–$220,000 per year, excluding equity or bonus targets. We intentionally offer a wide range to support flexibility around cash and equity. We believe strongly in equity ownership and want team members to share meaningfully in the impact they create.

If you’re outside this range, we still encourage you to apply – we take an individualized approach to compensation that considers the full picture.

Life at Ambience

Working at Ambience means opting into a high-ownership, high-trust environment built for people who want to grow fast, operate decisively and focus on work that matters. This could be the right place for you if you want to

 - Work on mission-critical AI technology that directly improves clinicians’ day-to-day lives and health system financial health across some of the most complex, high-stakes workflows in the world.

 - Join a “dream team” culture where we hire exceptional people, expect exceptional outcomes and invest deeply in feedback and continuous growth. We operate as a championship team, and that means being ok with hard, uncomfortable, ambiguous problems that lead to real greatness.

 - Operate with real ownership and accountability in an environment where there are no bystanders: If something is broken, we fix it! You will have meaningful autonomy and be expected to drive work to completion.

To help you do your best work, we pair these expectations with benefits intentionally designed to help you feel supported and safe at Ambience and beyond. Some of our key benefits include

 - Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage for you and your dependents

 - 401(k) with a company match of up to 3% of base salary

 - A remote-friendly culture (with a San Francisco HQ) and full equipment provisioning to ensure you can work effectively from wherever you’re based.

 - Parental leave to support your family needs

 - Annual company-wide off-sites, team off-sites and regular team lunches and all-hands gatherings, with travel, lodging and meals covered

 - Flexible time off with no annual cap, company-wide holidays and an annual holiday shutdown from December 24–January 1 designed to support real rest and long-term sustainability.

Ambience Healthcare is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, or any other legally protected status. We encourage applicants from all backgrounds to apply.

Ambience is committed to supporting every candidate’s ability to fully participate in our hiring process. If you need any accommodations during your application or interviews, please reach out to our Recruiting team at accommodations@ambiencehealthcare.com recruiting@ambiencehealth.com. We’ll handle your request confidentially and work with you to ensure an accessible and equitable experience for all candidates.


Ambience Healthcare has become aware of scams targeting jobseekers with fake jobs and even interviewing people. Our emails will always come from @ambiencehealthcare.com. We would never our ask candidates to download apps or make any form of payment(s). If you are contacted through WhatsApp, Telegram, similar but fake email domains, or asked to make a payment, these contacts are not legitimate. Report the issue immediately to LinkedIn and the FBI https://www.fbi.gov/how-we-can-help-you/scams-and-safety.
fields.title: Chief of Staff, Office of the President
